preview_text:
The model rates Turkey as favorite with a 50% win probability, while Paraguay is given a 20% chance to prevail. The gap reflects a meaningful but not decisive edge; Turkey enters as slight preference rather than strong favorite.

The 30% draw probability signals a competitive match where stalemate ranks as a plausible outcome. The spread across all three results—roughly 50-30-20—suggests an encounter unlikely to be one-sided, with genuine uncertainty around the finish line.

Model and market are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus places Turkey at 47%, a 3-percentage-point gap within normal variance. Both sources converge on draw likelihood near 28–29% and Paraguay around 24–25%. Polymarket prices track the consensus closely. All three estimates agree the match leans toward Turkey without strong conviction.
